bring-me-the-horizon~seen-it-all-before: “Seen It All Before” talks about Oliver’s depression, he was so much time in a sad life and with negative thoughts, now he’s tired of this. In a track-by-track interview with MetalFuckingRocks, drummer Matt Nicholls explained: It started out sounding like a really ‘90s, Euro thing that Jordan made.
nine-inch-nails~this-isn-t-the-place: This is the midpoint of the Add Violence EP.
filter~god-damn-me: This is the ninth track off of Filter’s 2002 album “The Amalgamut”
ocean-grove~hitachi: Running Touch, the writer/producer of this track and others by Ocean Grove: “It’s an ode to my old apartment, hitachi being the trains that used to run outside as i lived next to a train station.
asking-alexandria~alone-in-a-room: The song talks about Danny Worsnop’s in band and psychological situation. The music is a mixture of post-hardcore, pop and alternative rock.
pro8l3m~world-wide-web: Rozwój technologiczny świata spowodował, że jest opleciony globalną siecią – głównymi czynnikami na taki stan rzeczy jest powszechność telefonów, komputerów, czy internetu.
rammstein~stein-um-stein: The song is about an emotionally abusive relationship, which slowly takes shape in the three verses of the song. It is written from the bad guy’s point of view who speaks to the audience, which can make you feel uncomfortable.
polaris~above-my-head: This song has a bit of a double meaning in that it essentially parallels some of our anxieties about diving into the process of this album and feeling out of our depth, with a conversation between two people in a relationship who are scared about the uncertainty of their future.
serj-tankian~uneducated-democracy: Serj puts us in the role of a government in this great song that exposes the bad points of democracy and not choosing well.
haken~in-memoriam: In Memoriam explores the idea of mortality, with each verse touching upon the concept in different ways, only to ultimately arrive at the conclusion that acceptance forces itself as the only way to cope with death.
ministry~just-one-fix: The song documents the band’s heavy heroin use at the time and William S Boroughs even stars in the video.
northlane~heartmachine: Heartmachine is the fifth song from Northlane’s fourth studio album Mesmer, released on 24th March 2017.
babymetal~da-da-dance-feat-tak-matsumoto: It’s about a girl that really loves dancing and has powerful stamina when she hits the dance floor.
pendulum~slam: Slam was a double-sided single alongside with Out Here.
blood-red-shoes~it-s-getting-boring-by-t: Effectively a diss track to Blood Red Shoes home town of Brighton, “It’s Getting Boring By the Sea” was the track that made BRS darlings of the indie scene.
lisa~adamas: “ADAMAS” is a song recorded by LiSA.
system-of-a-down~ddevil: “DDevil” talks about plagiarism, the first verse is self-explanatory and ‘refrigerator’ is a metaphor for the ones that stole. But it also talks about the media, Double D’s referring to a woman’s huge breast.
limp-bizkit~leech-demo-version: “Leech” is the twelfth and second-to-last song on Limp Bizkit’s debut studio album Three Dollar Bill, Y'all$.
tool~pushit: “Pushit” is the 11th track on Tool’s 1996 sophomore effort Ænima, and it’s title is a combination of the words “push” and “shit.” One of their more lengthy and heavy tracks, it has been played mostly in Tool’s live shows pre-Lateralus, often interchanged with the version found on Salival.
bring-me-the-horizon~don-t-go: She first appeared on the opening track “Crucify Me.” Superficially, it tells the story of a failing relationship and dealing with depression. But actually, this song isn’t about relationships, depression and it’s not about Oli.
system-of-a-down~streamline: The song is about missing someone and how they regret not seeing them one last time or giving them a proper goodbye.
deftones~xenon: The 7th song off Gore, weaves through Chino Moreno’s mind as he is having issues with a girl, she is the one at fault, killing the fate of their relationship.
korn~everything-falls-apart: This is one of three songs from the upcoming album “The Serenity of Suffering,” due October 21st, 2016, which was played exclusively on SiriusXM Octane radio on October 19th, 2016.
nine-inch-nails~vessel: “Vessel” consists of heavily distorted drum loops, a wall of percussive synthesized sound, and ending improvization using a set batch of synthesized loops that distort and glitch.
tool~wings-for-marie-pt-1: “Wings for Marie” parts 1 and 2, as well as all of 10,000 Days, was dedicated to lead singer Maynard James Keenan’s mother Judith Marie Gridley.
faith-no-more~evidence: Evidence was the third single released on “King for a Day…”.
nothing-more~don-t-stop: “Don’t Stop” is the lead single from Nothing More’s 2017 studio record The Stories We Tell Ourselves alongside “Go To War”.
trivium~kirisute-gomen: Trivium’s lead vocalist Matt Heafy pays homage to his Japanese heritage in the first track off the album Shogun.
type-o-negative~the-dream-is-dead: Track 15 off Type O Negative’s Life Is Killing Me is the albums closer, that depicts another breakup-esque mood like others on the album (such as Anesthesia) with the protagonist alone and drunk on valentines day.
limp-bizkit~down-another-day: Fred is saying that the summer days are his favorite days since they represent for him the days that a relationship will end and then goes to the cold winter, comparing that the cold is when the relationships start to crumble.
slayer~jesus-saves: It is about the realization that you must be saved by Christ in order to enter the kingdom of Heaven.
dream-theater~vacant: Written by vocalist James LaBrie, “Vacant” is a piano-and-cello-driven ballad about the feelings of helplessness he and his wife felt when their daughter suffered a seizure and went into a coma. This song could be considered the prologue track of Stream of Consciousness, which is based around the same melodies and chord progressions.
europe~the-final-countdown: We just can’t use this.’ The song’s lyrics were inspired by David Bowie’s “Space Oddity”. Mic Michaeli said, “I made a brassy sound from the JX-8P and used a factory sound from the Yamaha, and just layered them together.” Read more of the 2005 interview with Joey Tempest here.
type-o-negative~green-man: On the surface, its about nature. More plainly, it’s about Peter Steele’s old job.
crystal-castles~transgender: Transgender is the 8th track off Crystal Castles' third studio album, (III).
